options: The aim of DEH is to boost exports of only selected districts.
India's Foreign Trade Policy 2023 reiterated the role of DEH.
DEH aims to promote exports by providing financial inclusion and logistical/infrastructural support.
explanation: The Districts as Export Hubs (DEH) initiative, driven by the DGFT, seeks to turn every district of India into an export hub by identifying products/services with export potential in each district and building the ecosystem (logistics, finance, infrastructure) to promote them. Statement b is wrong because the aim is to cover all districts, not "only selected districts." The launch around 2019, its reiteration in the Foreign Trade Policy 2023, and its focus on logistical/financial support (a, c, d) are correct.

subject: Economy
stem: Match (organisation – year of formation): A. European Union B. Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation (APEC) C. ASEAN D. Free Trade Area of the Americas (FTAA) |
explanation: Matching each body to its founding year: the European Union came into being with the Maastricht Treaty, 1993 (=4); APEC was formed in 1989 (=3); ASEAN is the oldest, established by the Bangkok Declaration of 1967 (=2); and the Free Trade Area of the Americas (FTAA) initiative was launched at the first Summit of the Americas in 1994 (=1). Hence A-4, B-3, C-2, D-1.

subject: Economy
stem: BREXIT refers to Great Britain leaving which body?
explanation: "BREXIT" is a portmanteau of "Britain" + "exit", referring to the United Kingdom's decision — taken in a June 2016 referendum — to withdraw from the European Union (d). The UK formally left the EU in January 2020. It has nothing to do with the IMF, the Commonwealth or the WTO.

subject: Economy
stem: About the Global Environment Facility Grant Agreement (Aug 2017, World Bank, Ecosystems Service Improvement Project), which statement is not correct?
options: The project size is about USD 25 million.
It is financed entirely by the World Bank from its GEF Trust Fund.
explanation: Under this 2017 Global Environment Facility (GEF) grant, India received about USD 25 million (a) financed from the GEF Trust Fund through the World Bank (c) for an Ecosystems Service Improvement Project implemented by the Ministry of Environment, Forest & Climate Change (d). The duration of such a project was about 5 years, not 15 — so statement b is the incorrect one. (A current-affairs item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Per the World Bank's Doing Business Report 2018, India's ranking improved (vs 2017) in which areas?
subs: 1. Paying taxes
2. Resolving insolvency
3. Starting a business
4. Getting electricity.
explanation: In the World Bank Doing Business Report 2018, India improved in Paying Taxes and Resolving Insolvency (statements 1 and 2), but did not improve in Starting a Business, so statement 3 is excluded - giving 1 and 2 only.

subject: Economy
stem: Which country is not a founding member of the New Development Bank?
explanation: The New Development Bank (NDB), headquartered in Shanghai, was set up in 2014 by the BRICS grouping — Brazil, Russia, India, China and South Africa — to finance infrastructure and sustainable-development projects in emerging economies, as an alternative to the World Bank/IMF. Canada is not a BRICS member and hence not a founding member (b).

subject: Economy
stem: Arrange in descending order per the Global Human Development Index 2019:
subs: 1. Germany
2. USA
3. South Africa
4. India.
explanation: The Human Development Index (HDI) ranks countries (0 to 1) on health, education and income. By HDI value: Germany (~0.94, "very high") leads, then the USA (~0.92, "very high"), then South Africa (~0.70, "medium"), with India lower (~0.64, "medium" lower end). So in descending order: Germany > USA > South Africa > India = 1, 2, 3, 4 (a).

subject: Economy
stem: Which body assists a country through the Extended Fund Facility?
options: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
explanation: The Extended Fund Facility (EFF) is a lending window of the International Monetary Fund (IMF), designed for countries with medium-term balance-of-payments problems rooted in structural weaknesses (it gives longer repayment periods than the IMF's standard facilities, in return for structural reforms). It is frequently in the news in connection with IMF bailout programmes (e.g. for Pakistan, Sri Lanka). So the IMF is the answer (b).

subject: Economy
stem: Which report defines Sustainable Development as "development that meets the needs of the present without compromising future generations"?
options: Agenda-21 of the Rio Earth Summit
explanation: This now-classic definition of sustainable development comes from the Brundtland Report, "Our Common Future" (1987), produced by the UN World Commission on Environment and Development (chaired by Norway's Gro Harlem Brundtland). It popularised the idea of balancing present needs against the ability of future generations to meet their own — the foundation for later milestones like Agenda 21 and the SDGs.

subject: Economy
stem: About India's rank in the Global Innovation Index-2023 (WIPO):
subs: 1. India was placed at 40th rank.
2. India has been on a rising trajectory over 2015-2023.
explanation: The Global Innovation Index (GII), published by the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO), ranks economies on innovation inputs and outputs. In GII 2023 India ranked 40th (statement 1), and India has been on a steady upward climb since 2015 — from around 81st to the 40th range (statement 2) — reflecting its expanding R&D, start-up and patent activity. Both statements are correct.

subject: Economy
stem: In the July 2018 cabinet decision, the percentage increase in MSP was maximum for which crop?
explanation: The Minimum Support Price (MSP) is the floor price at which the government promises to buy specified crops to protect farmers. In the July 2018 announcement the government adopted the "cost + 50%" formula (MSP = 1.5 × the A2+FL cost of production) for kharif crops, which delivered steep hikes. Among the listed crops, Jowar (hybrid) saw the largest percentage increase in its MSP. (A current-affairs/data item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which factor is not considered in determining the Minimum Support Price?
options: Price trends in international and domestic markets
explanation: MSPs are recommended by the Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP), which weighs factors such as the cost of production, demand and supply, domestic and international price trends, inter-crop price parity (so one crop isn't unduly favoured), terms of trade between agriculture and non-agriculture, and likely effect on consumers. The cost of living index (c) is not among the factors it considers — that index is used for wage/DA indexation, not for fixing crop support prices.

subject: Economy
stem: About the Public Distribution System:
subs: 1. Operated under the joint responsibility of Centre and States/UTs.
2. The FCI handles procurement, storage and transport of foodgrains to State Governments.
3. Both Centre and State jointly identify eligible beneficiaries.
4. Some States distribute additional items (pulses, edible oil) through PDS outlets.
explanation: The Public Distribution System (PDS) delivers subsidised foodgrains through ration shops under a shared Centre-State arrangement (statement 1). The Food Corporation of India (FCI) procures grain at MSP, stores it and transports it to the States (statement 2), and many States add extra items like pulses, edible oil, sugar and salt at their own cost (statement 4). Statement 3 is wrong: identifying eligible beneficiaries is the responsibility of the State governments (within the NFSA coverage), not a joint Centre-State task. Hence 1, 2 and 4.

subject: Economy
stem: Which is not an objective of the Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana (PMKSY)?
options: To achieve convergence of investment in irrigation at the field level
To expand cultivable area under irrigation
To protect farmers against crop failure due to natural calamities
explanation: PMKSY, launched in 2015 with the tagline "Har Khet Ko Pani" (water to every field) and "Per Drop More Crop," is an irrigation scheme. Its objectives are to converge irrigation investments at the field level (a), expand the area under assured irrigation (b) and improve water-use efficiency (c) through micro-irrigation (drip/sprinkler). Protecting farmers against crop loss from natural calamities (d) is the goal of crop insurance (the Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana), not PMKSY — so d is not its objective.

subject: Economy
stem: The Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana (PKVY), launched in 2015-16, is aimed at
options: making agriculture modern and globally competitive
improved access to irrigation and water efficiency
reducing agriculture's dependence on the monsoon
explanation: Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana (PKVY), launched in 2015-16, is the flagship scheme to promote organic farming. It works through a cluster approach — groups of farmers adopt chemical-free, traditional cultivation and get help with certification (the Participatory Guarantee System) and marketing of organic produce (c). It is not about global competitiveness (a), irrigation (b — that's PMKSY) or monsoon-proofing.

subject: Economy
stem: 1. The Government has discontinued the Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana.
subs: 2. The Government has approved continuation of the Restructured Weather Based Crop Insurance Scheme.
explanation: The Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY) — India's flagship crop-insurance scheme (launched 2016) that insures farmers against yield losses from natural calamities, pests and disease — has not been discontinued; it continues (and has been extended), so statement 1 is false. The Restructured Weather Based Crop Insurance Scheme (RWBCIS) — which insures against adverse weather parameters like rainfall, temperature and humidity — has indeed had its continuation approved (statement 2 correct). Hence 2 only.

subject: Economy
stem: The global initiative known as the "30 x 30 target" best reflects which statement?
options: At least 30% of the planet should be protected by 2030.
At least 30% of cultivated area under organic farming by 2030.
At least 30% of energy from renewables by 2030.
At least 30% of the poor lifted out of poverty by 2030.
explanation: "30x30" is the headline target of the Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework (2022): to bring at least 30% of the world's land and oceans under protection/conservation by 2030, to halt biodiversity loss (a). It is a conservation goal, not about organic farming, renewable energy or poverty. (Mis-filed here; an environment item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which statement about Anil Menon is correct?
options: An engineer who revolutionised organic farming
A diplomat in a UN agency
A physician selected by NASA to be an astronaut
explanation: Anil Menon is an Indian-American emergency-medicine physician (and former SpaceX flight surgeon) who was selected by NASA in its 2021 astronaut candidate class (c). (A current-affairs item, mis-filed here.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which is not an objective of MGNREGA?
options: Providing up to 100 days of skilled labour in a financial year
explanation: The Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA), 2005 legally guarantees every rural household up to 100 days of wage employment a year for adult members willing to do unskilled manual work. Its aims include livelihood security (c), creation of durable productive assets like ponds, roads and irrigation works (b), and women's empowerment (with at least one-third of workers to be women, d). Statement a is wrong because the work guaranteed is unskilled manual labour, not "skilled" labour.

subject: Economy
stem: Social Mobilisation and Institution Development (SMID) is a major component of
options: Atal Mission for Rejuvenation and Urban Transformation
explanation: The Deen Dayal Antyodaya Yojana – National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM) seeks to reduce poverty by organising the rural poor — especially women — into Self-Help Groups (SHGs) and their federations, then linking them to bank credit and livelihoods. The work of forming and strengthening these groups and institutions is its Social Mobilisation and Institution Development (SMID) component (a). (A governance item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which statement about the sugar industry of Peninsular India is not correct?
options: High yield per hectare of sugarcane
Most mills in the Peninsula are located mainly along the east coast
explanation: Peninsular India (especially Maharashtra and Karnataka) has overtaken the north as the sugar heartland because its tropical climate gives higher cane yields per hectare (a), higher sucrose content (b) and a longer crushing season (c) (cooler, longer growing period). Statement d is wrong: the mills are spread across the interior cane-growing tracts (the Deccan), not concentrated "mainly along the east coast." (A geography item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which statement about Antrix Corporation Limited is correct?
options: Under the administrative control of the Department of Space
Under the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
Commercial arm of the Department of Science and Technology
explanation: Antrix Corporation Limited is the commercial and marketing arm of ISRO — it sells ISRO's products and services (satellite launches, transponder leasing, remote-sensing data) globally. It operates under the Department of Space (b). (NewSpace India Limited, NSIL, is the newer commercial arm set up in 2019.) It is not linked to the DIPP, the Department of Science & Technology or the Health Ministry.

subject: Economy
stem: Growth in production of which core industry was negative during 2015-16?
explanation: Among the eight core industries, natural gas output recorded negative growth in 2015-16, weighed down by declining production from ageing domestic fields (a), whereas refinery products, fertilizer and coal posted positive growth that year. (A data item.)

subject: Economy
stem: The eBiz project (a Mission Mode Project under the National e-Governance Plan, guided by the DIPP) is implemented by
explanation: eBiz was a flagship single-window e-governance portal (one of the 31 Mission Mode Projects under the National e-Governance Plan) meant to provide government-to-business services — licences, clearances, registrations — through one online platform to improve ease of doing business. It was implemented in PPP mode by Infosys (b). (A governance item.)

subject: Economy
stem: As per the use-based classification of the Index of Industrial Production (IIP), the maximum weight is assigned to
explanation: The Index of Industrial Production (IIP) can be grouped by use into primary goods, capital goods, intermediate goods, infrastructure/construction goods, consumer durables and consumer non-durables. In the current (base 2011-12) series, primary goods carry the largest weight (~34%) — they include items like mining output, electricity and crude/refined products that feed the rest of industry. Hence a.

subject: Economy
stem: India achieved 10% ethanol blending ahead of target. Which greatly benefits from this?
explanation: Under the Ethanol Blended Petrol (EBP) Programme, ethanol is mixed into petrol to cut oil imports and emissions. In India ethanol is produced largely from sugarcane molasses and cane juice, so a higher blending target creates a big, assured market for the sugar industry (c) — giving mills an extra revenue stream and helping clear cane-payment dues to farmers. (A current-affairs item.)

subject: Economy
stem: About the cotton textile industry in India:
subs: 1. It has spread, over time, beyond Gujarat and Maharashtra.
2. Raw cotton is not a weight-losing raw material.
explanation: Historically India's cotton textile industry was concentrated in Gujarat (Ahmedabad) and Maharashtra (Mumbai), but with power availability, transport and markets it has since spread to Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, Punjab, UP, West Bengal and elsewhere (statement 1 correct). In location theory, raw cotton is a "pure" or non-weight-losing raw material — the cloth made from it retains essentially the cotton's weight — so the industry is not tied to the cotton-growing area and can locate near markets/ports (statement 2 correct). Hence both are correct.

subject: Economy
stem: About MSMEs:
subs: 1. They form a dynamic sector providing large employment.
2. They need low capital cost and help industrialize backward regions.
3. They contribute enormously to socio-economic development. How many are correct?
explanation: Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) are the backbone of the Indian economy: they are the second-largest employer after agriculture (statement 1), are labour-intensive with low capital requirements, so they can be set up in backward/rural regions and spread industrialisation (statement 2), and they contribute hugely to GDP, exports and broad-based socio-economic development (statement 3). All three statements are correct (d).

subject: Economy
stem: About the Indian Institute of Entrepreneurship (IIE):
subs: 1. It was established in 1999 in Guwahati.
2. Its main aim is training, research and consulting for small and micro enterprises.
explanation: The Indian Institute of Entrepreneurship (IIE), Guwahati is an autonomous body (under the Ministry of Skill Development & Entrepreneurship) set up to promote entrepreneurship, especially in the North-East, through training, research and consultancy for small and micro enterprises (statement 2 correct). Statement 1 is wrong on the year — IIE was established in 1993, not 1999. Hence 2 only.

subject: Economy
stem: Correct statements about the Saakshar Bharat scheme:
subs: 1. A centrally sponsored scheme launched in the Eleventh Plan.
2. Applies to women in particular and disadvantaged groups in general.
3. Applies to persons above the age of 10 years.
4. Anchored with Panchayati Raj Institutions/local self-government.
explanation: Saakshar Bharat (the National Literacy Mission's adult-literacy programme) was a centrally sponsored scheme launched in 2009 during the Eleventh Plan (statement 1), focused especially on adult women and disadvantaged groups — SCs, STs, minorities (statement 2) — and was implemented through Panchayati Raj Institutions/local bodies (statement 4). Statement 3 is wrong on the age: it targeted non-literate adults aged 15 years and above, not "above 10 years." Hence 1, 2 and 4. (A governance item.)

subject: Economy
stem: The Setu Bharatam Programme is for
options: building bridges across major rivers for goods transport
building bridges for safe and seamless travel on national highways
building roads to connect coastal areas
explanation: Setu Bharatam, launched in 2016, aims to make national highways free of railway level-crossings by 2019 by constructing rail over-bridges/under-bridges, and to rebuild/widen old and unsafe bridges — the goal being safe and seamless travel on national highways (b). It is about bridges on NHs, not river-freight bridges, coastal roads or engineer training. (A governance item.)

subject: Economy
stem: The projects under the Coastal Berth Scheme of the Sagarmala Programme are distributed over how many States?
explanation: Per PIB, Coastal Berth Scheme projects under Sagarmala were spread over eight states - Maharashtra, Andhra Pradesh, Goa, Karnataka, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Gujarat and West Bengal - not ten.

subject: Economy
stem: About the Bureau of Pharma PSUs of India (BPPI):
subs: 1. It is the implementing agency of the Pradhan Mantri Bhartiya Janaushadhi Pariyojana (PMBJP).
2. It is registered as an independent society under the Societies Registration Act, 1860.
explanation: The Pradhan Mantri Bhartiya Janaushadhi Pariyojana (PMBJP) opens "Janaushadhi Kendras" that sell quality generic medicines at low prices. Its implementing agency is the Bureau of Pharma PSUs of India (BPPI) (statement 1), which is registered as an independent society under the Societies Registration Act, 1860 (statement 2). Both statements are correct (c). (A governance item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Identify the scheme: launched for old-age income security, protecting those aged 60+ against falling interest income, providing an assured pension via LIC.
explanation: The Pradhan Mantri Vaya Vandana Yojana (PMVVY) is a pension scheme operated by LIC for senior citizens (60+) that pays a guaranteed/assured rate of return (pension) on the amount invested, shielding the elderly from falls in market interest rates on their savings (b). (The Rashtriya Vayoshri Yojana, d, by contrast, provides physical aids/assistive devices to poor senior citizens — a different scheme.) (A governance item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which is the earliest launched scheme of the Government of India?
explanation: The Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana (PMGSY) — which provides all-weather road connectivity to unconnected rural habitations — was launched in 2000, well before the others, which all came in 2014-15 (Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana, Saansad Adarsh Gram Yojana and DDU-GKY). So PMGSY (b) is the earliest. (A governance item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which is not a feature of the Ayushman Bharat scheme?
options: There is no cap on family size and age.
It includes pre- and post-hospitalization expenses.
A defined transport allowance per hospitalization is paid.
It provides a benefit cover of ₹10 lakh per family.
explanation: Under Ayushman Bharat – PM-JAY, the health cover is ₹5 lakh per family per year, not ₹10 lakh — so statement d is wrong. The genuine features are: no cap on family size or age (a) (the whole eligible family is covered), coverage of pre- and post-hospitalisation expenses (b), and a defined transport allowance per hospitalisation (c).

subject: Economy
stem: Correct statements about the Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana:
subs: 1. Part of the Government's poverty-reduction strategy.
2. A centrally sponsored scheme for rural development.
3. Provides connectivity in rural areas.
explanation: The PMGSY (2000) provides all-weather road connectivity to unconnected rural habitations (statement 3). It is a centrally sponsored rural-development scheme (statement 2), and because rural roads open up access to markets, schools, jobs and health care, it is regarded as part of the government's broader poverty-reduction strategy (statement 1). All three are correct (a). (A governance item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which is/are sub-mission(s) of the National Skill Development Mission (NSDM)?
subs: 1. Institutional training
2. Overseas employment
3. Leveraging of public infrastructure.
explanation: All three are sub-missions of the National Skill Development Mission - Institutional Training, Overseas Employment, and Leveraging Public Infrastructure - so the correct answer is 1, 2 and 3.

subject: Economy
stem: About the Second Five-Year Plan:
subs: 1. It was drafted under the leadership of K. N. Raj.
2. It proposed developing industries like electricity, railways, steel, machinery and communication in the public sector.
3. The drafters found balancing industry and agriculture very difficult.
4. The drafters found it really easy.
explanation: The Second Five-Year Plan (1956-61) was built on the Mahalanobis model, which prioritised rapid industrialisation led by the public sector in heavy industries — steel, machinery, power, railways, communications (statement 2). The planners genuinely struggled with the trade-off of how much to invest in industry versus agriculture (statement 3). Statement 1 is wrong — the architect was P. C. Mahalanobis, not K. N. Raj — and statement 4 contradicts 3. Hence 2 and 3.

subject: Economy
stem: Which set of States is at the bottom of the Composite SDG India Index 2020-21 (NITI Aayog)?
explanation: NITI Aayog's SDG India Index scores States on their progress towards the 17 Sustainable Development Goals. In the 2020-21 edition, the lowest-scoring States were Bihar, Jharkhand and Assam (a), reflecting weaker outcomes on poverty, health, education and infrastructure indicators. (A data-recall item; confirm against the original index report.)

subject: Economy
stem: The SDG India Index includes 17 SDGs for each State. Which is not included?
explanation: The 17 Sustainable Development Goals include Zero Hunger (Goal 2), Life Below Water (Goal 14) and Responsible Consumption and Production (Goal 12) (a, c, d). There is no SDG called "Reduced Unemployment." The relevant goal is Goal 10, "Reduced Inequalities," and employment is addressed within Goal 8 ("Decent Work and Economic Growth"). So "Reduced Unemployment" (b) is not an SDG.

subject: Economy
stem: Who was the Chairman of the Fourteenth Finance Commission?
explanation: The Finance Commission is a constitutional body (Article 280) that recommends how central taxes are shared between the Centre and States. The Fourteenth Finance Commission was chaired by Y. V. Reddy (a former RBI Governor); it made the landmark recommendation to raise the States' share of the divisible pool of central taxes from 32% to 42%, significantly boosting fiscal federalism. (C. Rangarajan chaired the Twelfth FC; Vijay Kelkar the Thirteenth.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which Indian cricketer was not inducted into the ICC Cricket Hall of Fame till July 2018?
explanation: The ICC Cricket Hall of Fame honours legends of the game. By July 2018, the Indians already inducted included Sunil Gavaskar, Bishan Bedi, Kapil Dev, Anil Kumble (2015) and Rahul Dravid (inducted 2018). Sachin Tendulkar was inducted only in 2019, so as of July 2018 he had not yet been inducted (c). (A current-affairs/sports item.)

subject: Economy
stem: The Sasakawa Award of the United Nations is given for work in the field of
explanation: The UN Sasakawa Award for Disaster Risk Reduction is the most prestigious international recognition in the field of disaster risk reduction (a) — given to individuals or institutions for active efforts in reducing disaster risk in their communities. (Funded by the Nippon Foundation; mis-filed here under economy.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which is not a dimension of the Human Development Index?
options: Access to banking and other financial provisions
explanation: The Human Development Index (HDI), created by the UNDP, is built on three dimensions: a long and healthy life (measured by life expectancy, a), knowledge (mean and expected years of schooling, b) and a decent standard of living (GNI per capita, d). Access to banking/financial services (c) — a financial-inclusion indicator — is not one of the HDI's dimensions.

subject: Economy
stem: Per UNDP's Human Development Report 2020, in which HDI components has India improved?
subs: 1. Life expectancy at birth
2. Expected years of schooling
3. GNI per capita
4. Mean years of schooling.
explanation: The HDI is composed of four underlying indicators across its three dimensions: life expectancy at birth, expected years of schooling, mean years of schooling, and GNI per capita. Over recent years India recorded improvement on all four — people are living longer, children expect and complete more years of schooling, and income per head has risen — so all four (d) have improved (lifting India's overall HDI value).

subject: Economy
stem: Who is the beneficiary target group of the Garib Kalyan Rojgar Abhiyan?
explanation: The Garib Kalyan Rojgar Abhiyan was launched in June 2020, during the COVID-19 lockdown, specifically to provide employment and livelihood opportunities to the migrant workers who had returned to their villages after losing urban jobs (b). It bundled the works of multiple ministries into a campaign across selected districts in six states with high return-migration. (A current-affairs item.)

subject: Economy
stem: The computation of poverty in terms of Monthly Per Capita Consumption Expenditure (MPCE) based on the Mixed Reference Period was recommended by the
explanation: India estimates poverty using Monthly Per Capita Consumption Expenditure (MPCE) from NSSO surveys. The Tendulkar Committee (2009) updated the methodology — it moved to the Mixed Reference Period (MRP) for capturing consumption (different recall periods for different items), shifted away from calorie-only norms to a broader basket including health and education, and used the all-India urban poverty line as the reference (b). The Lakdawala, Dandekar-Rath and Alagh committees were earlier in the lineage.

subject: Economy
stem: Per the National MPI (NITI Aayog), a household is considered deprived if:
subs: 1. a single member is undernourished.
2. the BMI of a woman aged 15-49 is below 18.5 kg/m².
explanation: In the National Multidimensional Poverty Index, the "Nutrition" indicator (under the Health dimension) treats a household as deprived if any member is undernourished (statement 1) — and undernourishment for adult women aged 15-49 is identified by a Body Mass Index below 18.5 kg/m² (statement 2). So both statements describe the deprivation criterion correctly (c).

subject: Economy
stem: Regarding poverty in India:
subs: 1. The poverty line is fixed using a minimum level of food, clothing, footwear, fuel/light, education and medical requirements.
2. The accepted average calorie requirement is 2400 (rural) and 2100 (urban) calories per person per day.
explanation: India's poverty line is built on a basket of minimum needs — not just food but also clothing, footwear, fuel & light, education and medical care (statement 1). The long-accepted calorie norms underlying the food component are 2400 kcal per person per day in rural areas and 2100 kcal in urban areas (statement 2) — urban people need slightly fewer calories because of less physically demanding work. Both statements are correct (a).

subject: Economy
stem: About the Tendulkar Committee (2009) poverty estimates:
subs: 1. It used an all-India urban poverty-line basket as the reference to derive both rural and urban poverty.
2. It anchored the poverty line to the official food-calorie norms.
explanation: The Tendulkar Committee broke with the past in two ways: it adopted the all-India urban poverty-line basket as a common reference for both rural and urban poverty (statement 1, correct), and it moved away from anchoring the poverty line to calorie norms, instead using a broader consumption basket that included spending on health and education (so statement 2, which says it kept the calorie anchor, is wrong). Hence 1 only.

subject: Economy
stem: Which dimension is not included in the Human Development Index?
explanation: The HDI uses four indicators: life expectancy at birth (health), mean years of schooling and expected years of schooling (education), and GNI per capita (income). The "mortality rate" (d) is not used as a separate HDI indicator — health is captured by life expectancy, which already reflects mortality conditions. Hence d is the odd one out.

subject: Economy
stem: The Rangarajan Committee methodology for the poverty line incorporated:
subs: 1. A food component.
2. A normative level of expenditure for essential non-food items.
3. A behaviourally determined expenditure for other non-food items.
explanation: The Rangarajan Committee (2014), set up to review the (controversial) Tendulkar poverty line, built its poverty line from three parts: a food component (meeting calorie, protein and fat norms together, statement 1), a normative allowance for essential non-food items — education, clothing, conveyance, house rent (statement 2) — and a behaviourally determined component for other non-food expenditure based on observed spending (statement 3). All three (c) were incorporated, which is why its poverty estimates were higher than Tendulkar's.

subject: Economy
stem: The mismatch in the regional or occupational pattern of job vacancies and the pattern of worker availability results in
explanation: Structural unemployment arises from a mismatch between the skills/location of available workers and the requirements/location of available jobs — for example, vacancies in IT in one city while unemployed textile workers sit in another. It is more persistent than cyclical unemployment. Disguised unemployment (b) is where more people work than are needed (common in agriculture); cyclical unemployment (d) is caused by downturns in the business cycle. So the described mismatch is structural (a).

subject: Economy
stem: Which statement about the National Rural Employment Guarantee Act, 2005 is correct?
options: It ensures 175 days of employment to needy rural persons.
It gives higher wages to women workers.
People are generally given both skilled and unskilled jobs.
It is implemented only in rural areas of India.
explanation: Under MGNREGA (2005), employment is guaranteed for up to 100 days a year (not 175, so a is wrong), the work is unskilled manual labour (so c is wrong), and wages are equal for men and women (so b — "higher wages to women" — is wrong). The Act applies only in rural areas (an urban equivalent does not exist at the national level) — so statement d is the correct one.

subject: Economy
stem: Unemployment that occurs due to changes in technology or in the demand for particular products is called
explanation: Structural unemployment results from long-term shifts in the structure of the economy — new technology making certain skills/jobs obsolete (e.g. automation), or a permanent fall in demand for a product/industry. Workers' existing skills no longer match available jobs. This differs from frictional unemployment (a) — the short, normal gap between jobs — and cyclical unemployment (c) — caused by temporary downturns. So technology/demand-driven joblessness is structural (b).

subject: Economy
stem: About the PLFS report 2023-24:
subs: 1. Agriculture's employment share rose from about 44% (2017-18) to about 46% (2023-24).
2. The share of female workers in agriculture increased over 2017-18 to 2023-24.
explanation: The Periodic Labour Force Survey (PLFS), conducted by the NSO, is India's main source of employment-unemployment data. Its 2023-24 findings showed that, against the long-run expectation of workers shifting out of farming, agriculture's share of total employment actually rose from about 44% to 46% (statement 1) — partly a post-COVID reversal — and the share of female workers in agriculture also increased (statement 2), reflecting rising rural female participation concentrated in farming. Both are correct (a); the options referencing statements "3/4" are spurious as only two statements exist.

subject: Economy
stem: Which surveys are conducted by the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation?
subs: 1. Survey on Social Consumption: Health
2. Comprehensive Modular Survey: Telecom and ICT skills
3. Periodic Labour Force Survey
4. Annual Survey on Unincorporated Enterprises.
explanation: The Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI), through the National Statistical Office (NSO), conducts India's large-scale official surveys. All four listed are NSO/MoSPI surveys — the Survey on Social Consumption: Health, the Comprehensive Modular Survey on Telecom and ICT skills, the Periodic Labour Force Survey (PLFS) and the Annual Survey of Unincorporated Sector Enterprises (ASUSE). Hence all four (a).

subject: Economy
stem: Match author with work: A. Sumit Sarkar B. Shahid Amin C. Ranajit Guha D. Bipan Chandra |
subs: 1. The Rise and Growth of Economic Nationalism in India
2. A Rule of Property for Bengal
3. The Swadeshi Movement in Bengal, 1903-1908
4. Event, Metaphor, Memory — Chauri Chaura, 1922-1992.
explanation: These are well-known works of modern Indian historiography. Sumit Sarkar wrote The Swadeshi Movement in Bengal (3); Shahid Amin wrote Event, Metaphor, Memory: Chauri Chaura (4); Ranajit Guha (founder of Subaltern Studies) wrote A Rule of Property for Bengal (2); and Bipan Chandra wrote The Rise and Growth of Economic Nationalism in India (1). Hence A-3, B-4, C-2, D-1. (A history item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Who is the Chairman of the Chiefs of Staff Committee?
options: The member who has been the longest on the Committee
explanation: The Chiefs of Staff Committee (COSC) comprises the chiefs of the Army, Navy and Air Force. Under the system in force when this question was set (before the Chief of Defence Staff post was created in 2019), the chairmanship rotated to the senior-most service chief — the one with the longest tenure on the committee (d). (A defence item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Correct statements about the Shiromani Gurudwara Prabandhak Committee (SGPC):
subs: 1. It began as the political wing of the Singh Sabhas in the late 19th century.
2. It was formed in 1920 as part of the Akali movement.
3. It was founded to reclaim control of Sikh shrines from government-manipulated loyalist committees.
4. It formed the Akali Dal to coordinate Jathas to reclaim the shrines.
explanation: The SGPC was formed in 1920 as part of the Akali (Gurdwara Reform) movement (statement 2) to wrest control of Sikh gurdwaras from the hereditary/loyalist mahants and government-backed managers (statement 3), and it set up the Akali Dal to coordinate the volunteer bands (Jathas) that carried out this reclamation (statement 4). Statement 1 is wrong — the SGPC was not the political wing of the late-19th-century Singh Sabhas. Hence 2, 3 and 4. (A history item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which is not a component of the Revenue Receipts of the Union Government?
explanation: Revenue receipts are inflows that neither create a liability nor reduce an asset — taxes (corporate tax, a), and non-tax revenue like dividends/profits from PSUs and the RBI (b) and interest receipts on loans (d). Disinvestment receipts (c) come from selling government equity in PSUs — this reduces a government asset, so it is a capital receipt, not a revenue receipt. Hence c is the odd one out.

subject: Economy
stem: The 7.6% growth rate of the Indian economy in 2015-16 was based on
options: Gross National Product at market prices
Gross Value Added at constant prices
Gross Domestic Product at market prices
Gross Domestic Product at constant prices
explanation: A "growth rate" is always a real growth figure — it must strip out inflation, which means valuing output at constant (base-year) prices. So the 7.6% figure for 2015-16 was GDP growth at constant prices (d). At market/current prices the number would mix in price changes (nominal growth) and would not represent real expansion of output.

subject: Economy
stem: Amartya Sen was awarded the Nobel Prize for his contribution to
explanation: Amartya Sen won the 1998 Nobel Memorial Prize in Economics for his contributions to welfare economics — including social choice theory, the measurement of poverty and inequality, and his work on the causes of famines (showing famines stem from failures of entitlements/distribution, not just food shortage). His ideas also underpin the Human Development Index. So the field is welfare economics (b).

subject: Economy
stem: The Ryotwari experiment in land revenue was started by
explanation: The Ryotwari system of land revenue — under which the government settled revenue directly with the individual cultivator (ryot), without intermediary zamindars — was first introduced experimentally by Captain Alexander Read (with Thomas Munro) in the Baramahal region of Madras in the 1790s, and later extended across Madras and Bombay (b). (A history item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Who chaired the committee to investigate the 1 November 2017 accident at the Feroze Gandhi Thermal Power Plant, Unchahar (UP)?
explanation: The Ministry of Power committee on the Unchahar (NTPC) accident was chaired by Shri P. D. Siwal. Dr. L. D. Papney was Member and Convenor, not the chairman - so the chairman is P. D. Siwal (option d).

subject: Economy
stem: Who is the Chairman of the Defence Planning Committee set up in April 2018?
options: The Chief of the Army Staff
explanation: The Defence Planning Committee (DPC), set up in April 2018 as a permanent body to give defence and national-security planning a more integrated, long-term direction, is chaired by the National Security Advisor (NSA) (b). Its members include the three service chiefs and the defence, expenditure and foreign secretaries. (A defence item.)

subject: Economy
stem: The sub-prime crisis is associated with which event?
explanation: The sub-prime crisis refers to the 2007-08 US housing/mortgage meltdown, in which loans given to "sub-prime" (high-risk) borrowers defaulted en masse, collapsing mortgage-backed securities and toppling major financial institutions (e.g. Lehman Brothers). It triggered the global financial crisis and the "Great Recession" — so it is associated with economic recession (a).

subject: Economy
stem: Which canon of taxation was not advocated by Adam Smith?
explanation: Adam Smith, in The Wealth of Nations, laid down four canons of taxation: equality (taxes in proportion to ability to pay, a), certainty (the taxpayer should know how much, when and how to pay, b), convenience (levied at a time and manner convenient to the taxpayer, c) and economy (low cost of collection). The canon of fiscal adequacy (d) — that taxes should yield enough revenue — was added by later economists (e.g. Bastable), not by Adam Smith. Hence d.

subject: Economy
stem: What was the Dutt-Bradley thesis?
options: The INC Working Committee's role in independence
The Socialist party's foremost role in the anti-imperialist struggle
A Communist party document holding that the National Congress could play a great and foremost part in an anti-imperialist people's front
explanation: The Dutt-Bradley thesis (1936), authored by Rajani Palme Dutt and Ben Bradley of the British Communist Party, was a Communist document arguing that the Indian National Congress could become the principal vehicle of a broad "anti-imperialist people's front" uniting all anti-colonial forces (d). It signalled the communists' shift toward working within the Congress. (A history item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Who gave the concept of Human Development?
explanation: The concept of Human Development — that development should be measured by the enlargement of people's choices and capabilities, not just income — was pioneered by the Pakistani economist Mahbub-ul-Haq, who launched the UNDP's first Human Development Report and the HDI in 1990 (b). He worked closely with Amartya Sen (a), whose capability approach provided the theoretical foundation, but the operational concept/index is credited to Haq.

subject: Economy
stem: The Ministry of Heavy Industries and Public Enterprises consists of
options: the Department of Heavy Industry and the DIPP
the Department of Public Enterprises and the DIPP
the DSIR and the Department of Heavy Industry
the Department of Heavy Industry and the Department of Public Enterprises
explanation: At the time, the Ministry of Heavy Industries and Public Enterprises comprised two departments — the Department of Heavy Industry (overseeing central public-sector heavy-engineering, auto and capital-goods enterprises) and the Department of Public Enterprises (the nodal department for PSU policy) (d). (The DIPP — now DPIIT — is under the Commerce Ministry, not this one.) (A governance item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Who is the Chairman of the Economic Advisory Council to the Prime Minister (EAC-PM)?
explanation: The Economic Advisory Council to the Prime Minister (EAC-PM) is an independent body that advises the PM on economic matters. At the time of this question it was chaired by the economist Bibek Debroy (b). (A current-affairs item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Formulation of policy on Intellectual Property Rights (IPRs) is the responsibility of
options: the Ministry of Law and Justice
the Department of Science and Technology
the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade
the Ministry of Human Resource Development
explanation: In India, IPR policy (patents, trademarks, designs, copyrights, GIs) is formulated and coordinated by the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) under the Ministry of Commerce and Industry — which framed the National IPR Policy 2016 and oversees the patent and trademark offices (c). (A governance item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Who gave evidence before the Joint Select Committee on the Government of India Bill, 1919 in favour of female franchise?
subs: 1. Mrs. Annie Besant
2. Mrs. Sarojini Naidu
3. Mrs. Hirabai Tata.
explanation: When the Government of India Bill, 1919 (which led to the Montagu-Chelmsford Reforms) was examined, a delegation of Indian women argued for women's right to vote. Annie Besant, Sarojini Naidu and Hirabai Tata all gave evidence before the Joint Select Committee in support of female franchise (d). (A history item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which statement about the appointment of Members of Parliamentary Committees is correct?
options: The Members are appointed or elected on a motion made and adopted, or nominated by the Speaker (Lok Sabha) or Chairman (Rajya Sabha).
explanation: Members of Parliamentary Committees are constituted in two ways — they are elected by the House (through proportional representation by single transferable vote, on a motion moved and adopted) or nominated by the Speaker (Lok Sabha)/Chairman (Rajya Sabha) — depending on the committee. Option d captures this dual mechanism correctly. (A polity item.)

subject: Economy
stem: The percentage by which the money the borrower pays back exceeds the money borrowed is called
explanation: The nominal interest rate is simply the stated percentage by which the amount repaid exceeds the amount borrowed, in money terms, before adjusting for inflation (b). The real interest rate (c) is the nominal rate minus inflation (the true increase in purchasing power). The bank rate (a) is a specific central-bank lending rate, and terms of credit (d) is the broader set of loan conditions (rate, collateral, documentation, repayment).

subject: Economy
stem: Which motion cannot be made while introducing an ordinary Bill in Parliament?
options: That the Bill be taken into consideration
That the Bill be circulated for eliciting public opinion
That the Bill be referred to a Select Committee
That the Bill be referred to a Joint Committee of the House without the concurrence of the other House
explanation: When a Bill is introduced, the member-in-charge can move that it be taken into consideration (a), referred to a Select Committee of the House (c), or circulated to elicit public opinion (b). A Bill cannot be referred to a Joint Committee of both Houses without the concurrence (agreement) of the other House — a joint committee involves both Houses, so the other House must consent (d is therefore the motion that cannot be made unilaterally). (A polity item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which statements are correct?
subs: 1. The Speaker may permit a Member to address the House in their mother tongue if they cannot adequately express in Hindi or English.
2. Business of Parliamentary Committees is transacted in Hindi or English.
3. The minutes of Parliamentary Committees are prepared invariably in Hindi or English.
explanation: On language in Parliament: business is normally conducted in Hindi or English, but the Speaker/Chairman may allow a member who cannot adequately express themselves in either to speak in their mother tongue (statement 1). The business of parliamentary committees is transacted in Hindi or English (statement 2), and their minutes/records are prepared in Hindi or English (statement 3). All three are correct (a). (A polity item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Under which name did the Russian Federation participate in the Tokyo Olympics 2020?
explanation: Because of sanctions imposed for a state-sponsored doping scandal, Russia was barred from competing under its national name, flag and anthem. Its athletes therefore took part in the Tokyo 2020 Olympics under the banner of the "ROC" — the Russian Olympic Committee (a) — rather than as "Russia." (A current-affairs/sports item.)

subject: Economy
stem: 1. The Committee on Estimates is an ad hoc committee of Parliament.
subs: 2. The Committee on Railway Convention is a standing committee of Parliament.
explanation: Parliamentary committees are of two types — standing (permanent, constituted regularly) and ad hoc (temporary, for a specific purpose). The Estimates Committee is one of the three great financial standing committees (not ad hoc), so statement 1 is wrong. The Railway Convention Committee is in fact an ad hoc committee (not standing), so statement 2 is also wrong. Hence neither statement is correct (d). (A polity item.)

subject: Economy
stem: What is a "Unicorn Company" often mentioned in Indian news?
options: Any privately held startup valued over $1 billion
A PSU to be merged with another PSU
Privatization of a loss-making State-owned company
A foreign MNC operating in India with an Indian partner
explanation: In the start-up world, a "unicorn" is a privately held start-up whose valuation has crossed US$1 billion (a) — the term, coined by venture capitalist Aileen Lee, captures how rare such companies once were. India has the world's third-largest number of unicorns (Flipkart, Paytm, BYJU'S, etc.). It has nothing to do with PSU mergers, privatisation or MNC joint ventures.

subject: Economy
stem: In India, which body is responsible for implementing the Government's policy on all matters concerning telecommunications?
explanation: The Digital Communications Commission (DCC) — formerly the Telecom Commission — is the apex body in the Department of Telecommunications responsible for formulating and implementing the government's telecom policy and overseeing the sector (a). It should not be confused with TRAI (b), which is the independent regulator that recommends tariffs and rules, or with TCIL (d), a PSU consultancy.

subject: Economy
stem: Which statement about the Public Accounts Committee (PAC) is not correct?
options: It examines the Finance Accounts of the Government of India.
Fifteen members are elected by the Lok Sabha from amongst its members.
The Chairperson of the Committee is elected by its members.
The Speaker decides on continued membership in case of dual-committee membership.
explanation: The Public Accounts Committee (PAC) audits government spending using the CAG's reports. It has 22 members — 15 from the Lok Sabha (elected) and 7 from the Rajya Sabha (b is correct), and it examines the appropriation and finance accounts (a is correct). Its Chairperson is appointed by the Speaker (by convention from the Opposition), not elected by the committee's own members — so statement c is the incorrect one. (A polity item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which statement about the Solicitor General of India (SGI) is not correct?
options: The SGI is the Government's chief legal advisor.
The SGI is the secondary law officer of the country.
The post of SGI is a constitutional post.
The SGI is appointed by the Appointments Committee of the Cabinet.
explanation: Only the Attorney General of India is a constitutional post (Article 76); the Solicitor General (and Additional Solicitors General) are statutory/executive posts, created by government, not the Constitution — so statement c is incorrect. The SGI is the second-ranking law officer of the country (b), assists the AG and is the government's senior legal advisor/advocate (a), appointed by the Appointments Committee of the Cabinet (d). (A polity item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which committee does not have members from the Rajya Sabha?
options: The Committee on the Welfare of SCs and STs
explanation: Of the three financial committees, the Estimates Committee is unique in being composed entirely of Lok Sabha members (30) — it has no Rajya Sabha representation (c). By contrast the Public Accounts Committee and the Committee on Public Undertakings each have members from both Houses (15+7 and 15+7), and the Committee on the Welfare of SCs & STs also draws from both Houses. (A polity item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which was not part of the action programme of the Non-Cooperation Movement?
options: The Congress organisation was to reach down to the village/mohalla level.
Boycott of government-affiliated schools and colleges.
Taking control of the State's law-and-order machinery by the Congress Working Committee.
Surrender of titles and honours given by the government.
explanation: The Non-Cooperation Movement (1920-22) was, by definition, about withdrawing cooperation from the colonial state through peaceful means — surrendering titles and honours (d), boycotting government schools, colleges, courts and foreign cloth (b), and strengthening the Congress organisation down to the village/mohalla level (a). It did not aim to seize the State's law-and-order machinery (c) — that would be a violent insurrection, contrary to its non-violent, non-cooperationist character. Hence c is the odd one out. (A history item.)

subject: Economy
stem: Which are included in the M1 definition of money?
subs: 1. Reserves
2. Currency
3. Time deposits
4. Demand deposits.
explanation: M1 (Narrow Money) is the most liquid measure of money — funds available for immediate transactions. It comprises currency with the public (2), demand deposits (4) (and "other deposits" with the RBI). Time deposits (3) are not part of M1 — they are locked in for a period and are added only in the broader M3. "Reserves" (1) (banks' balances with the RBI) belong to reserve money, not M1. Hence M1 = currency + demand deposits = 2 and 4 (c).

subject: Economy
stem: About the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO):
subs: 1. One of its goals is to promote a new democratic, fair and rational international political and economic order.
2. Iran is a permanent member of the SCO.
explanation: The Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO) is a Eurasian political, economic and security grouping (members include China, Russia, India, Pakistan and Central Asian states). Among its stated objectives is promoting a "new democratic, fair and rational international political and economic order" (statement 1, correct). Iran became a full (permanent) member in 2023 (statement 2, correct). Hence both are correct (c). (A current-affairs/IR item.)

subject: Economy
stem: 1. A rupee Vostro account is one that an Indian bank holds for a foreign bank in rupees, enabling domestic banks to provide international banking services.
subs: 2. Insurance density (average premium per capita) does not fully capture the financing gap on premature death because most Indian insurance products are savings-linked with a small protection component.
explanation: A Vostro account ("your account with us") is an account a domestic (Indian) bank maintains on behalf of a foreign bank, in the domestic currency (rupees) — Special Rupee Vostro Accounts have been used to enable rupee-based international trade settlement (statement 1, correct). On insurance, "insurance density" (premium per capita) overstates protection because most Indian life-insurance products are savings/investment-linked with only a thin pure-protection element, so the true protection (mortality) gap is larger than density suggests (statement 2, correct). Both are correct (c).

subject: Economy
stem: About the Central Vigilance Commission (CVC):
subs: 1. The Central Vigilance Commissioner is appointed by the President of India.
2. The committee recommending the appointment includes the Minister of Home Affairs.
3. In certain circumstances the Leader of the single largest opposition group in the Lok Sabha can be on the committee. How many are correct?
explanation: The Central Vigilance Commission (CVC) is the apex integrity/anti-corruption watchdog (statutory, given teeth after the Vineet Narain case). The Central Vigilance Commissioner is appointed by the President (statement 1) on the recommendation of a three-member committee comprising the Prime Minister, the Minister of Home Affairs (statement 2) and the Leader of the Opposition in the Lok Sabha — and where there is no recognised Leader of the Opposition, the leader of the single largest opposition group/party in the Lok Sabha takes that place (statement 3). All three statements are correct (d). (A polity item.)
